zh.Thorpe, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾索普 聖 彼得
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Thorpe於大約1206年出生在英國林可爾史爾索普 聖 彼得時，她的父親John de Thorpe39歲，母親Margery de Creke18歲。她於1225年之前與William de Skipwith在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1316年在英國林可爾史爾享年111歲。

nameMeanings.name: Thorpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): from Middle English thorp(e) ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English thorp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from any of several places called Thorp(e), which are most frequent in Yorkshire, the Midlands, and East Anglia. Compare Throop .

variants.name: Trupp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Hermann, Reinhold.

English (Herefordshire and Monmouthshire): variant of   Thorpe  , or from a place so named, such as Thrupp (Berkshire, Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire), Thrupe (Somerset), Throop (Dorset, Hampshire, Somerset), and Throope (Wiltshire).

variants.name: Throop
meanings: English: from Middle English throp ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English throp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from Throop in Hampshire, Throope in Wiltshire, Thrup in Oxfordshire, or any of the places called Thrupp in Berkshire, Gloucestershire, and Northamptonshire. Compare Thorpe .

variants.name: Tharpe
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Thorpe  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Thorp
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Thorpe  .

variants.name: Troup
meanings: Scottish (eastern): habitational name from a place in the parish of Gamrie, near Banff. The placename is unexplained, perhaps a Pictish cognate of Welsh trwp ‘vessel, dish, basin’.
Possibly also English, a metathesized form of   Thorpe  .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Thorpe was born about 1206, in Thorpe St Peter, Lincolnshire, England, her father, John de Thorpe, was 39 and her mother, Margery de Creke, was 18. She married William de Skipwith before 1225, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1316, in Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 111.

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Thorpe(이)가 대략 1206년 영국 링코실르 소 스트 피터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Thorpe(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery de Creke(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1225년 이전 영국에서 William de Skipwith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1316년 영국 링코실르에서 111세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Thorpe родилась около 1206 г. в местности таурп ст питир, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Thorpe было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery de Creke, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Skipwith перед 1225 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.
